detail: GWAS Catalog association with p=1e-9. Effect: OR/Beta=1.0257254. Initial sample: 449,734  European ancestry individuals. Replication sample: 248,098 European ancestry individuals.
whyItMatters: This common-variant association is matched locally against the uploaded genotype and should be treated as a tendency signal, not a prediction.
notes: Generated from bulk GWAS Catalog data; effect size and ancestry transferability require review.

title: Smokes normal (lower) number of cigarettes if a smoker
summary: Smokes normal (lower) number of cigarettes if a smoker.
detail: Less nicotine dependence. Also smokes each cigarette less deeply. Therefore there is a reduced risk of lung cancer and a reduced (0.8x) risk of Peripheral Artery Disease. This doesn't affect whether people start smoking though.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.

detail: This SNP is associated with a change in the protein encoded by the HEXA gene, which leads to a false positive blood test for Tay-Sachs Disease. In other words, the blood test from an individual with this genotype will indicate the person is a carrier for Tay Sachs disease, even though this SNP is not actually one that leads to the disease. Note however that the person may still carry (on their other chromosome 15) a "true" Tay Sachs causing allele.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.
conditions: likely to lead to a false positive blood test for Tay Sachs

Note however that the person may still carry (on their other chromosome 15) a "true" Tay Sachs causing allele.
